title: Medulla Neurotransmitters
prompts: What neurotransmitters are used in the medulla?

messages.content: What neurotransmitters are used in the medulla?

Short answer:
- The medulla uses multiple neurotransmitters: acetylcholine (cholinergic), glutamate (glutamatergic), and GABA (GABAergic). Some neurons even co-express multiple transmitters.

Representative examples from VFB data for medulla-associated neurons:
- Cholinergic (acetylcholine)
  - MeVP3 (FBbt_20005420) — presynaptic in the medulla; cholinergic. [VFB page for MeVP3](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_20005420)
  - T4 (FBbt_00003731) — postsynaptic here; cholinergic. [VFB page for T4](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00003731)
  - TM1 (Tm1; FBbt_00003789) — presynaptic in the medulla; cholinergic. [VFB page for Tm1](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00003789)
  - TM20 (Tm20; FBbt_00003808) — presynaptic in the medulla; cholinergic. [VFB page for Tm20](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00003808)
  - ML1 (FBbt_00052137) — postsynaptic in the medulla; cholinergic. [VFB page for ML1](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00052137)
  - Note: TM3 is also cholinergic and, in fact, shows co-expression with GABAergic traits. [VFB page for TM3](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00003791)

- Glutamatergic (glutamate)
  - Rh3TmY (FBbt_00110986) — presynaptic in the medulla; glutamatergic. [VFB page for Rh3TmY](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00110986)
  - Rh5TmY (FBbt_00110988) — presynaptic in the medulla; glutamatergic. [VFB page for Rh5TmY](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00110988)

- GABAergic (GABA)
  - Medulla tangential neuron vGATMtnew (FBbt_00110980) — presynaptic in the medulla; GABAergic. [VFB page for vGATMtnew](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00110980)
  - Transmedullary neuron vGATTmnew (FBbt_00110981) — presynaptic in the medulla; GABAergic. [VFB page for vGATTmnew](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00110981)

- Note on co-transmission:
  - TM3 (FBbt_00003791) is listed as cholinergic and GABAergic, indicating possible co-transmission in that cell type. [VFB page for TM3](https://virtualflybrain.org/?q=FBbt_00003791)

If you’d like, I can pull a broader list of medulla-associated neurons categorized by transmitter type (or by specific subregions or circuits) and provide a clean table with IDs, neurotransmitters, and links to each VFB page.

Follow-up questions (to narrow the query):
- Do you want a complete list of all medulla neurons with a specific transmitter (e.g., all glutamatergic medulla neurons)?
- Are you interested in neurons with documented co-transmission (e.g., cholinergic plus GABAergic) within the medulla?
- Should I map transmitter types to known medulla circuits (e.g., connections to T4/T5, or optic lobe outputs) for a quick circuit overview?
